name: Stanley Cowell
abstract: Stanley Cowell (May 5, 1941 – December 17, 2020) was an American jazz pianist and co-founder of the Strata-East Records label.

concerts.label: The New York Jazz Repertory Company: Duke Ellington 75th Birthday Salute

works.t: Black, Brown and Beige: Come Sunday
I Got It Bad (and That Ain't Good)

concerts.label: New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Music of George Russell
Kool Jazz Festival: Now's The Time
Kool Jazz Festival: Charlie Haden's Liberation Music Orchestra/Philly Joe Jones' Dameronia
New Jazz: John Blake and Stanley Cowell

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: first it was Reggie Workman and myself, and Stanley Cowell, a very fine pianist. Then, we added a few more people who became the core of the collective black artists organization.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: Later on, when you were leading the quintet with Harold Land, you had a variety of pianists in that group: Chick Corea, Stanley Cowell, Joe Sample.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: with the joining of Stanley Cowell, who was the president and part owner of that label, Strata East, he played the kalimba.
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: I had Sir Roland Hanna on the piano and Stanley Cowell, who is a regular pianist with us, on some tracks.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tolliver
about_quotes.evidence: my dear Stanley Cowell and I were so close you know, every day

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Stanley Cowell went there... [INTERVIEWEE] ...I think the piano player that you mentioned before. [INTERVIEWER] Stanley? [INTERVIEWEE] No, he played with – I’m looking right at him in my mind, and I can’t think of his name.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
